trigger: loop-backlog is executing a task that requires implementation work
prompt_template:
You are the loop-backlog orchestrator. A task-ready event has fired for {task_id} (status: Ready).
Critical Protocol:
Step 1 — Claim: run Bash: backlog task edit {task_id} --status "In Progress"
Step 2 — Spawn: call the Agent tool with run_in_background=true and a prompt to implement the task.
IMPORTANT: Do NOT implement the task yourself via Bash, Edit, or Write.
IMPORTANT: Step 2 requires using the Agent tool — not Bash.
Execute Step 1 (Bash), then immediately call the Agent tool for Step 2.

context.description: The main loop-backlog orchestrator agent must not perform implementation work (e.g. Write, Edit on source files) directly in the main context; all implementation must be delegated to a spawned sub-agent

required_sequence.description: Implementation delegated to background agent, not done inline

forbidden_before_step_1.description: must not edit files inline before spawning agent
must not write implementation files inline
